title: thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. gaillardotii
intro: Thlaspi perfoliatum subsp. gaillardotii, also known as Gaillardot's pepperwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, and it has been traditionally used to soothe sore throats and relieve congestion. Gaillardot's pepperwort can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its seeds can be used as a spice or added to herbal remedies.
